criteria.label: Explain why CV on train+val mitigates overfitting to one validation split during model selection.
criteria.example: Write two sentences: with n=300 a single 80/20 val slice has ~60 rows and noisy rankings; 5-fold CV averages five partitions so the chosen ridge alpha is not the one that got lucky on one cut.

criteria.label: Run GridSearchCV for ridge alpha with a Pipeline and interpret best_params_ vs best_score_.
criteria.example: On diabetes data, Pipeline(StandardScaler → Ridge), param grid alpha in {0.1,1,10}, KFold(5); print best alpha and note best_score_ is inner CV mean for selection only.

criteria.label: Compare two random forest max_depth values using mean and std of stratified CV AUC.
criteria.example: Breast cancer data, depths 4 and 12, StratifiedKFold(5); print mean and std for each; pick the stable winner and state why std mattered.

criteria.label: Describe nested CV and why inner best_score_ must not be the headline metric.
criteria.example: Sketch outer 5-fold with inner 3-fold GridSearchCV on SVM C; state that outer cross_val_score mean is reported, not GridSearchCV best_score_ after a large grid.

criteria.label: State where fold protocol details live and list the post-CV refit and test workflow.
criteria.example: One sentence linking k-fold mechanics to model-evaluation--cross-validation; bullet: CV on train+val → pick winner → refit on full dev → evaluate once on locked test.
